channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: NSW ECEC sector grows as monitoring activity rises and new service applications decline
item.description: New regulatory data for Quarter 3 (January–March) of the 2025–26 financial year shows the NSW early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector continues to expand, while regulatory oversight and compliance activity increases across the state.
item.content:encoded: New regulatory data for Quarter 3 (January–March) of the 2025–26 financial year shows the NSW early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector continues to expand, while regulatory oversight and compliance activity increases across the state.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Annual wage review 2026: new wage and allowance determinations released ahead of 1 July changes
item.description: The Fair Work Commission (FWC) has released the final determinations for the Annual Wage Review 2026, confirming increases to minimum wages and expense-related allowances across Australia's modern awards.
item.content:encoded: The Fair Work Commission (FWC) has released the final determinations for the Annual Wage Review 2026, confirming increases to minimum wages and expense-related allowances across Australia's modern awards.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: NSW introduces enhancements to Assessment and Rating from 1 July 2026
item.description: NSW early childhood education and care (ECEC) services will see significant changes to the Assessment and Rating (A&R) process from 1 July 2026, with the NSW Department of Education introducing a strengthened, year-round approach to continuous improvement, service readiness and regulatory assurance.
item.content:encoded: NSW early childhood education and care (ECEC) services will see significant changes to the Assessment and Rating (A&R) process from 1 July 2026, with the NSW Department of Education introducing a strengthened, year-round approach to continuous improvement, service readiness and regulatory assurance.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Stop washing childhood away: why mud matters in toddler programs and in acknowledgement of International Mud Day, June 29
item.description: Anyone who has worked with toddlers will recognise the scene. It has rained overnight, and the outdoor environment is wet. Puddles have formed, the soil has softened, and toddlers are already making their way towards them. One child stirs mud with a stick; another presses their hands into wet soil and a third repeatedly transports water from the tap to a patch of muddy ground. For toddlers, muddy patches seem to exert an almost irresistible pull; for adults, however, the same scene can evoke a r
item.content:encoded: Anyone who has worked with toddlers will recognise the scene. It has rained overnight, and the outdoor environment is wet. Puddles have formed, the soil has softened, and toddlers are already making their way towards them. One child stirs mud with a stick; another presses their hands into wet soil and a third repeatedly transports water from the tap to a patch of muddy ground. For toddlers, muddy patches seem to exert an almost irresistible pull; for adults, however, the same scene can evoke a r
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Rebuilding trust in Early Childhood Education and Care: listening to families, upholding children’s rights
item.description: In early childhood education and care (ECEC), we often speak about quality frameworks, compliance, ratios, and learning outcomes. Yet beneath these structures sits something far more fragile and far more important: trust.
item.content:encoded: In early childhood education and care (ECEC), we often speak about quality frameworks, compliance, ratios, and learning outcomes. Yet beneath these structures sits something far more fragile and far more important: trust.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.description: Mapped: Find NSW’s gold-star childcare centres near you - Daily Telegraph Sydney
item.content:encoded: Mapped: Find NSW’s gold-star childcare centres near you - Daily Telegraph Sydney
item.dc:creator: Daily Telegraph Sydney

item.title: Fair Work wage rise puts early childhood workforce in focus
item.description: The Fair Work Commission's latest wage decision has delivered a pay increase for millions of award-reliant workers while drawing renewed attention to the workforce challenges facing female-dominated sectors such as early childhood education and care.
item.content:encoded: The Fair Work Commission's latest wage decision has delivered a pay increase for millions of award-reliant workers while drawing renewed attention to the workforce challenges facing female-dominated sectors such as early childhood education and care.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.content:encoded: Generation Y is quietly reshaping early childhood education and their influence is only growing - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Everyone experiences loss and grief – but that doesn’t make it any easier to talk with kids about it
item.description: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.content:encoded: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Marlee Silva – National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day 2026 Ambassador

item.content:encoded: Marlee Silva is the National Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Children’s Day 2026 Ambassador! Find out more in the Ambassador Video!

item.title: The Breadwinner: Comedy doesn’t need flawless dads, but we do need competent ones
item.description: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.content:encoded: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.description: SNAICC responds to Worker Retention Payment extension, NT and Queensland child protection scrutiny, Aboriginal Family-Led Decision Making and Children’s Day.

item.title: Worker Retention Payment extended to 2028: what early childhood providers need to know - The Sector

item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Childcare workers have been guaranteed a pay bump. What’s the fine print?
item.description: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Investing in educators means investing in children
item.description: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC commends the Federal Government backing hardworking early education and care (ECEC) workers by extending the Worker Retention Payment. Catherine Liddle, CEO of SNAICC said the extension of the 15% wage increase is a welcome and necessary investment in the ECEC workforce.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Toy Story 5 pits traditional toys vs a tablet. In real life, families can combine the two
item.description: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.content:encoded: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 12 June 2026

item.title: Report shows how young people want to use their voice for change
item.description: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.content:encoded: Young Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people want authentic representation, decision-making power and community-led structures that deliver real outcomes, according to a landmark national report released today.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples ready to work in partnership for a safer future for Queensland children
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children CEO Catherine Liddle has urged the Queensland Government to work in genuine partnership with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people and communities in reforming the State’s child protection system.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to proposed NT child protection laws and the Children’s Commissioner’s report, and announces Marlee Silva as 2026 Children’s Day Ambassador.

item.title: SNAICC warns proposed NT child protection laws will make children less safe
item.description: SNAICC has warned that the Northern Territory Government's proposed NT child protection laws will make children less safe.

item.title: SNAICC in the News | 29 May 2026
item.description: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to NT child protection reforms, National Sorry Day, AbSec’s call for systemic change, the launch of the Bushland Animal Yoga Cards, and continued Budget coverage.
item.content:encoded: In this week’s SNAICC in the News, SNAICC responds to NT child protection reforms, National Sorry Day, AbSec’s call for systemic change, the launch of the Bushland Animal Yoga Cards, and continued Budget coverage.

item.description: ACECQA Incident Response Template strengthens child safe governance expectations across ECEC - The Sector
item.content:encoded: ACECQA Incident Response Template strengthens child safe governance expectations across ECEC - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: From Country to Classroom: SNAICC Launches Culturally Grounded Learning Tools
item.description: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.content:encoded: SNAICC – National Voice for our Children has launched a unique culturally informed early Childhood Education and Care resource for early childhood workers. The Bushland Animal Yoga Cards’ offer ACCO and non-Indigenous ECEC services a resource that incorporates movement with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages, with native Australian animals representing various poses and movements.
item.dc:creator: SNAICC

item.title: To improve children’s mental health, start by supporting their parents
item.description: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.content:encoded: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Is baby talk bad? Why ‘parentese’ actually helps babies learn language
item.description: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.content:encoded: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation
